title: The Sonnets CLIV - The little Love-god lying once asleep
content:
The little Love-god lying once asleep,
    Laid by his side his heart-inflaming brand,
    Whilst many nymphs that vow�d chaste life to keep
    Came tripping by; but in her maiden hand
    The fairest votary took up that fire
    Which many legions of true hearts had warm�d;
    And so the general of hot desire
    Was, sleeping, by a virgin hand disarm�d.
    This brand she quenched in a cool well by,
    Which from Love�s fire took heat perpetual,
    Growing a bath and healthful remedy,
    For men diseas�d; but I, my mistress� thrall,
    Came there for cure and this by that I prove,
    Love�s fire heats water, water cools not love.
meaning: About: William Shakespeare (baptised 26 April 1564 � 23 April 1616)[a] was an English poet and playwright, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language and the world's pre-eminent dramatist. He is often called England's national poet and the "Bard of Avon" (or simply "The Bard"). His surviving works consist of 38 plays, 154 sonnets, two long narrative poems, and several other poems. His plays have been translated into every major living language and are performed more often than those of any other playwright.
